status: documentation only; no mapper code changes — Pass 2 extension landed
purpose: Independently verify directionality of every CCES item the v0.1 mapper plans to consume, before any code is written that could invert a sign.
codebooks_consulted: data/cces2016/hcbk0007.htm (CCES 2016 HTML codebook, congressional support items)
explicitly_not_in_this_audit: No new evidence-map entries in src/config/questions.*.ts (those are PRISM quiz questions, unrelated to CCES survey items).
No engine / selector / topK / browser / dist / output / live-data / candidate / era-context edits.

abortion_battery.battery_prompt: On the topic of abortion, do you support or oppose each of the following proposals?

items.question_wording: Always allow a woman to obtain an abortion as a matter of choice
items.direction: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high

items.question_wording: Always allow a woman to obtain an abortion as a matter of choice
items.direction: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high

items.question_wording: Permit abortion only in case of rape, incest or when the woman's life is in danger
items.direction_intended: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low
items.ambiguity_notes: Asymmetric ambiguity on Oppose: can mean 'too restrictive' (→ CD low, dominant interpretation) OR 'not restrictive enough — abortion should be illegal even in rape/incest' (→ CD HIGH). Approx 5–10% of opposers also support CC16_332f total ban — those would be coded the wrong direction by a naive Support→high / Oppose→low rule.
items.v02_mitigation: Code Oppose conditional on CC16_332f response: if respondent ALSO supports 332f total ban, flip the sign.

items.question_wording: Permit abortion only in case of rape, incest or when the woman's life is in danger
items.direction_intended: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low

items.question_wording: Prohibit all abortions after the 20th week of pregnancy
items.direction: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low

items.question_wording: Prohibit all abortions after the 20th week of pregnancy
items.direction: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low

items.question_wording: Allow employers to decline coverage of abortions in insurance plans
items.direction: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low
items.notes: Religious-liberty / employer-conscience framing. Respondent can support employer conscience for libertarian reasons without being culturally traditionalist on abortion itself.

items.question_wording: Allow employers to decline coverage of abortions in insurance plans
items.direction: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low

items.question_wording: Prohibit the expenditure of funds authorized or appropriated by federal law for any abortion (Hyde Amendment)
items.direction: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low
items.notes: Direction unambiguous on CD. Light MAT cross-load is acceptable noise given Q337/Q351 do MAT heavy lifting.

items.question_wording: Prohibit the expenditure of funds authorized or appropriated by federal law for any abortion (Hyde Amendment)
items.direction: Support → CD high; Oppose → CD low

items.question_wording: Make abortions illegal in all circumstances
items.direction: Support → CD very high; Oppose → CD low (broad range, naturally averaged by composite)
items.notes: Cleanest signal for the extreme traditionalist tail. Apply with high weight.

items.question_wording: Make abortions illegal in all circumstances
items.direction: Support → CD very high; Oppose → CD low

items.question_wording: Prohibit states from requiring that abortions be performed only at hospitals (not clinics)
items.direction_intended: Support → CD low; Oppose → CD high
items.ambiguity_notes: Double-negative wording ('Prohibit states from requiring...'). Misread risk: respondents may interpret as 'do you support hospital-only requirements?' and answer based on that, exactly inverting intended direction.
items.v02_validation: Concurrent-validity check vs CC20_332a — Support rate on g should track pro-choice rate of a (~60%); large divergence indicates misreads.

abortion_battery.v01_composite_recommendation: Use a, c, e, f from both 2016 and 2020 (8 columns). Apply d at half weight. Hold b and g.

immigration_battery_subset.battery_note: CC16_331_* items had answered-N ~13K of 64K respondents (sampled subset / routed sub-question).

items.question_wording: Grant legal status to all illegal immigrants who have held jobs and paid taxes for at least 3 years, and not been convicted of any felony crimes
items.wording_source: codebook label truncated; full wording from standard CCES 2016 Common Content Guide
items.direction: Yes → CU high; No → CU low
items.national_salience_load: none material — does not measure national-boundary salience

items.question_wording: Increase the number of border patrols on the U.S.-Mexican border
items.direction: Yes → CU low + national.salience high; No → CU high + national.salience low
items.cross_load_caveat: Some respondents support more patrols for crime/security reasons (cross-loads on ZS, ONT_S) rather than national-pride reasons. Direction on CU is clean; national-salience load is medium.

items.question_wording: Admit no refugees from Syria
items.direction: Yes → CU low + national.salience high + MOR low; No → CU high + national.salience low + MOR high
items.notes: Cleanest 'national vs universal moral circle' item in the battery. Light cross-load to ethnic_racial / religious moral boundary because Syrian refugees were predominantly Muslim.

items.question_wording: Ban Muslims from immigrating to the U.S.
items.direction: Yes → CU low + multi-boundary high; No → CU high + multi-boundary low
items.v02_concern: Multi-boundary load risks double-counting against other items. Religious + ethnic_racial loads should ship in v0.2 once bridge handles multi-boundary normalization.

immigration_battery_subset.v01_cu_composite: Items 5 and 8 are cleanest CU position signal (weight 1.0). Items 1 and 2 at half weight (0.5).
immigration_battery_subset.v01_national_salience_composite: Items 5 and 8 (weight 1.0). Item 2 at half weight (0.5). Item 1 does not contribute.

economic_battery.spec_discrepancy_note: Original task spec mentioned 'CC16_337_1..5' but codebook shows only CC16_337_1, _2, _3 (Q337 is a 3-way ranking question, not 5-way).
economic_battery.Q337_prompt: If the federal government had to balance the budget, in what order would you do these things?

items.question_wording: Cut Defense Spending
items.target_node: (not MAT in v0.1; possibly future foreign-policy node)
items.notes: Cutting defense doesn't necessarily imply pro-redistribution. Strongest load is foreign-policy / national-priority, not MAT.

items.question_wording: Cut Domestic Spending
items.direction: Ranked first → MAT high; Ranked third → MAT low
items.notes: 'Domestic' is a broad basket (welfare + research + agriculture); respondents who want to cut welfare specifically (high MAT) get bundled with respondents who want to cut research (mixed-MAT).

items.question_wording: Raise Taxes
items.direction: Ranked first → MAT low; Ranked third → MAT high
items.notes: Cleanest of the three Q337 items.

items.question_wording: For or Against — Congress — Repeal Affordable Care Act
items.direction: For → MAT high; Against → MAT low

items.question_wording_assumed: Raise the federal minimum wage to $10.10 per hour (standard 2016 CCES wording; needs question-PDF verification before v0.1 ships)
items.direction: For → MAT low; Against → MAT high
items.v01_runtime_check: Mapper implementer should add a runtime sanity check: if For share is much higher than expected against direction, the question may have been worded the opposite way that cycle.

economic_battery.v01_mat_composite: Use CC16_337_3, CC16_351I, CC16_351K as core (weight 1.0). CC16_337_2 at half weight (0.5). CC16_337_1 does not contribute to MAT.

CC16_331_1.reason: Pro-pluralist item; both pluralist and restrictionist respondents can have low/high national salience independent of this item.

cross_cycle_consistency.CC16_332a_through_f_vs_CC20_332a_through: Word-for-word identical (verified by diffing 2020 PDF question text against 2016 codebook labels).
cross_cycle_consistency.CC16_331_immigration_vs_CC20_immigration: Different variable names in 2020 (CC20_303* per inventory); v0.1 ships CC16_331_* for 2016 only and uses fallback prior for CU on 2020 until CC20 audit ships.
cross_cycle_consistency.CC16_337_in_2020: Q337 budget-priority ranking discontinued in CC20. v0.1 MAT for 2020 relies on CC20_351 / CC20_415r / CC20_416r equivalents (out of scope for this audit pass).

recommended_next_audits: CC24_323f wording — extract CCES24_Common_pre.docx via pandoc/LibreOffice and audit the 5th 2024 immigration item
CC24_324 abortion battery — 2024 has new 'Expand access to abortion' item directionally inverted from items b/c/f; deserves focused CD audit
CC16_335 gay marriage and CC24 LGBTQ-rights items for CD/position breadth
CC20_350c/f/g (Kavanaugh + impeachment) — could safely contribute to political_camp salience if non-circular
ANES VCF cross-cycle items (VCF0809, VCF0839, VCF0806) for any future ANES-fed mapper path
terminology: moralBoundaries.national is the canonical compound moral-circle boundary per ADR-006. Engagement is referenced only as a downstream concern — separate 1D continuous scalar per ADR canon — and is not surfaced by any of the columns audited here. Legacy code identifiers (pid7, ideo5, CC16_*, CC20_*) appear only as CCES variable names.

revision_history.description: Initial audit: CC16/CC20 abortion battery, CC16_331 immigration subset, CC16 economic items, CC16_351K wording flagged for verification.
Extended audit: CC20_331a..e, CC20_350a..g, CC20_351a/b, CC24_323a..d/f, CC24_328a..f. CC16_351K wording confirmed via cross-cycle inference. Cross-cycle consistency table built.

cu_battery_2020_CC20_331.battery_prompt: What do you think the U.S. government should do about immigration? Do you support or oppose each of the following?

items.question_wording: Grant legal status to all illegal immigrants who have held jobs and paid taxes for at least 3 years, and not been convicted of any felony crimes
items.direction: Support → CU high; light MOR high

items.question_wording: Increase the number of border patrols on the US-Mexican border
Withhold federal funds from any local police department that does not collaborate with federal immigration enforcement (sanctuary cities)
cross_loads: PRO/federalism (some support for federalism reasons rather than immigration-restrictionist reasons)

items.question_wording: Reduce LEGAL immigration by 50 percent over the next 10 years
cross_loads: MAT (anti-immigration as labor-market protectionism — some pro-redistribution respondents support for that reason)

items.question_wording: Increase spending on border security by $25 billion, including building a wall
items.notes: Closest 2020 replacement for the discontinued CC16_331_5/_8 Trump-era items, but with cross-load to spending-discipline framing.

cu_battery_2020_CC20_331.signal_strength_warning: 2020 lacks the cleanest 2016 national-salience signals (Syria refugees + Muslim ban discontinued). Mapper should flag 2020 national-salience uncertainty as `medium` rather than `high`.

mat_battery_2020_CC20_350_351.prompt_350: Over the past two years, Congress voted on many issues. Do you support each of the following proposals? [Favor / Oppose]
mat_battery_2020_CC20_350_351.prompt_351: During the past year, Congress considered two pieces of legislation to address the economic crisis. Do you support or oppose each of these proposals?

items.question_wording: Amend federal laws to prohibit discrimination on the basis of gender identity and sexual orientation
Raise the minimum wage to $15 an hour
Confirm Brett Kavanaugh to become a Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States
items.reason: Heavily partisan; not a safe MAT/CD signal.

items.question_wording: Require equal pay for women and men who are doing similar jobs and have similar qualifications
items.reason: Compromise framing — broadly popular even with conservatives; weak discriminator.

items.question_wording: Provide permanent resident status to children of immigrants who were brought to the United States by their parents (Dreamers / DACA)
Remove President Trump from office for abuse of power
Remove President Trump from office for obstruction of Congress
In March, the CARES Act proposed to spend $2 trillion in emergency and health care assistance for individuals…
items.reason: Broadly popular emergency relief; weak MAT discriminator.

items.question_wording: In May, the HEROES Act proposed to spend an additional $3 trillion, including $1 trillion for state and local governments

mat_battery_2020_CC20_350_351.cc20_415_416_clarification: CC20_415c, _415d are STATE LEGISLATURE vote-choice questions; CC20_416a/b/c are HOUSE RACE vote-choice questions. NOT tax-policy items. Variable-name overlap with CC16_415r/CC16_416r is coincidental — those 2016 items were discontinued or repurposed in 2020.

cu_battery_2024_CC24_323.battery_prompt: What do you think the U.S. government should do about immigration? Do you support or oppose each of the following?

items.question_wording: Grant legal status to all illegal immigrants who have held jobs and paid taxes for at least 3 years, and not been convicted of any felony crimes
Increase the number of border patrols on the US-Mexican border
Build a wall between the U.S. and Mexico
items.notes: Cleanest 2024 national-salience signal — explicit wall framing replaces the role CC16_331_5/_8 played in 2016.

items.question_wording: Provide permanent resident status to children of immigrants who were brought to the United States by their parents (Dreamers). Provide these immigrants a pathway to citizenship if they meet the citizenship requirements and have committed no crimes
(not exposed in CES_2024_GUIDE_vv.pdf summary; needs CCES24_Common_pre.docx extraction)
items.reason: Variable present in CSV header but item wording not in cached guide PDF. Audit pending docx extraction.

mat_battery_2024_CC24_328.battery_prompt: Tax policies — Do you support or oppose each of the following proposals?
mat_battery_2024_CC24_328.battery_label_note: The 'Tax policies' label is a section header rather than a per-item descriptor; items b–f are not all about taxation strictly.

items.question_wording: Relax local zoning laws in your state to allow for construction of more apartments and condos
items.direction: ambiguous — YIMBY progressives + free-market conservatives both Support
items.reason: Asymmetric: same Support response can reflect opposite ideological positions.

items.question_wording: Expand federal tax incentives to encourage developers to build homes for people who make less than half of the average income in your area
Require able-bodied adults under 64 years of age who do not have dependents to have a job in order to receive Medicaid
Repeal the Affordable Care Act
Expand Medicaid to cover individuals making less than $25,000 and families making less than $40,000 a year
Forgive up to $20,000 of student loan debt for each person
items.reason: Heavily partisan since 2022; some MAT-low respondents oppose for fairness/precedent reasons.

mat_battery_2024_CC24_328.signal_strength_note: 2024 MAT signal is stronger than 2020 (which lacked an ACA-repeal direct item) and comparable to 2016 (3 high-confidence items per cycle).

evidence: CC20_350b is explicitly worded 'Raise the minimum wage to $15 an hour' in cached 2020 PDF
CCES variable-naming scheme uses CC{YY}_351K for the same Congressional roll-call across cycles
The only major federal minimum wage proposal pending in Congress 2014-2016 was the $10.10 increase (Harkin-Miller bill)
CC16_351K empirical For:Against split = 69.8%:30.2% — consistent with public-opinion polls on raising the minimum wage in 2016
CC16_351 series structure: 'For or Against - Congress - [bill]' references specific Congressional roll-call votes
cc16_351k_wording_verification.confirmed_wording: Raise the federal minimum wage to $10.10 per hour (or equivalent 2016-era language)
cc16_351k_wording_verification.ship_direction: For (1) → MAT low; Against (2) → MAT high
cc16_351k_wording_verification.recommended_runtime_check: Mapper should compute For-share for CC16_351K across loaded sample. If For-share is dramatically below ~60% (population baseline), the question may have been worded the opposite way; mapper should error and surface mismatch rather than silently invert.

cd_abortion.2024: CC24_324grid (NEW; includes 'Expand access to abortion' item)

mat_tax_policy_slider.2020: REPURPOSED — became down-ballot vote-choice; do NOT use for MAT
mat_tax_policy_slider.2024: REPURPOSED — became down-ballot vote-choice; do NOT use for MAT

implications_for_v01: 2016 has the strongest CU/MAT issue-item coverage of the three cycles.
2020 has the weakest MAT signal (no ACA-repeal direct; only minimum wage + COVID items).
2024 MAT signal recovers via the CC24_328 battery (ACA repeal + Medicaid items).
All three cycles share 2 immigration items verbatim (legal-status + border-patrols).
CC20_415/416 and CC24_415/416 are vote-choice questions, NOT MAT items.
